name: Pulse — Morning digest
description: Daily 07:00. Overdue commitments, today's meetings, Horizon, updated projects, candidate new projects, team inbox.

steps.label: Generate morning digest
steps.prompt:
Generate today's morning digest. Use /pulse-projects for project memory context.

1. Resolve <PULSE_HOME> via $env:OneDriveCommercial\Documents\Pulse, falling back to $env:OneDrive\Documents\Pulse, then $env:USERPROFILE\Documents\Pulse.
2. Find overdue commitments across all <PULSE_HOME>\projects\*.yaml — only those with status: open AND due before today AND due_confidence: explicit. Sort by days overdue. Inferred due dates never count as overdue.
3. List today's meetings via m365_list_events. Match each to a project by attendee company / subject keywords / stakeholder name and note prep status. Flag any meeting with no project link as [NO PREP].
4. Call m_get_horizon_actions. If it errors with LokiGraphQLError or any error, omit the Horizon section silently — don't fail.
5. List projects whose updated_at is within the last 24h, with a one-line 'what changed' from the latest timeline entry.
6. Detect candidate NEW projects per the /pulse-projects discovery rule (3+ mentions, 2+ source types, 1+ actionable) from recent emails/chats — list as candidates, do NOT auto-create.
7. Count pending agent_requests in <PULSE_HOME>\jobs\pending\.
8. If <PULSE_HOME>\intel\<today>.md exists, link to it.

Write a ≤50-line digest to <PULSE_HOME>\digests\<today>.md AND surface in chat. Order: overdue → today's meetings → Horizon → updated projects → candidate new projects → team inbox → intel pointer. Drop empty sections entirely. Specifics over summaries — names, exact day counts, amounts. Don't inflate.

name: Pulse — Workday triage
description: Every 30 min on weekdays from 09:00. Classify unread email/chats; draft replies (never auto-sends); update commitments where evidence shows progress.

steps.label: Triage unread inbox
steps.prompt:
Triage unread email and chats. Use /pulse-projects for project context.

1. Resolve <PULSE_HOME>.
2. Pull m365_list_emails (unread, last 24h, top 50) and m365_list_chats (top 10 with unread, expand via m365_list_chat_messages).
3. Classify each item: reply_needed | escalation | commitment_update | meeting_prep | info | fyi. Default to info when uncertain. Don't over-classify as reply_needed — false positives waste user attention.
4. Cross-reference with project YAMLs at <PULSE_HOME>\projects\ — surface project slug for matched items. When evidence shows a commitment was completed/delivered, update the project per /pulse-projects.
5. For each reply_needed, draft a response using m365_create_draft (saves to Drafts; user edits and sends). Match the user's tone — concise, direct. Reference details from project memory by name where available.
6. NEVER auto-send. Always show drafts and let the user confirm.

Output: scannable summary in chat. Sections: 🚨 escalations → ✉️ replies needed (with drafts) → 📅 next 24h meetings → 📋 commitments updated → 📥 sweep candidates. End with one-liner: 'Reply to draft 1?' or similar.

ALWAYS emit at least one line. If there's nothing actionable, emit a brief one-liner like '(no new escalations or replies needed)' — Clawpilot's automation runner treats empty output as failure.

name: Pulse — Daily intel brief
description: Daily 09:00. RSS digest filtered for relevance to your work. Edit the prompt to point at your own feeds and competitors.

steps.label: Generate intel brief
steps.prompt:
Run today's RSS intelligence brief using Playwright MCP browser tools. Resolve <PULSE_HOME>.

Feeds (edit to your own):
- https://news.google.com/rss/search?q=AI+agents+LLM&when:2d&hl=en-US
- https://techcrunch.com/category/artificial-intelligence/feed/
- https://arstechnica.com/ai/feed/
- https://openai.com/blog/rss.xml

Competitors / topics to watch (edit to your own):
- Google Gemini — pricing, enterprise wins, new services
- OpenAI — model releases, pricing, enterprise wins
- Anthropic — model releases, enterprise wins
- AI Agents, LLM, foundation models

1. Read <PULSE_HOME>\.intel-state.json if present — skip URLs already surfaced in prior runs.
2. For each feed, browser_navigate and parse <item> (RSS) or <entry> (Atom). On feed failure, log and continue.
3. Drop articles older than 48h.
4. Score relevance: HIGH = competitor + watch term, OR matches a project memory customer + meaningful event. MEDIUM = topic keyword in title. LOW = topic keyword in body only. DROP = generic AI hype, listicles, opinion pieces.
5. Cap at 15 articles. Group by Competitor moves → Topic → Industry/customer (link to project slug if matched). Per article: 1–3 lines — source, what happened, why it matters in this user's role.

Write ≤40 lines to <PULSE_HOME>\intel\<today>.md AND surface in chat. Update <PULSE_HOME>\.intel-state.json with run timestamp and the URLs included.

Never fabricate articles. Don't editorialize — report what happened, not what to think. Specifics: names, products, pricing, dates.

name: Pulse — Team collaboration poll
description: Every 5 min. Process incoming agent_request and agent_response YAMLs in jobs/pending. Silent when inbox is empty. Enable after sharing your OneDrive folder with at least one teammate.
